title: ceropegia bourneae
intro: Ceropegia bourneae, also known as the 'Ring Finger Vine' or 'Twinflower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and improving respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, Ceropegia bourneae is typically made into a decoction or infusion by boiling its roots and leaves in water, which can then be used to treat a range of ailments.
